title: 奉命谋杀
summary:
Calgary meets with Dr. McMaster to discuss the fallout of the message he delivered to the Aigle family, noting that their reaction lacked the expected anger or gratitude. Calgary expresses a desire to better understand the family, observing a disconnect between the public perception of the deceased, Mrs. Aigle, as a selfless and charitable woman and the reality of her relationship with her adopted children. He specifically questions the nature of the "problem child" or juvenile delinquent within the household.

Dr. McMaster explains that Mrs. Aigle’s kindness was excessive and driven by an obsession. Because she never experienced biological pregnancy or childbirth, her maternal instinct became all-consuming. She provided the children with highly controlled environments, including sterilized milk, tested water, and calculated caloric intakes, while simultaneously neglecting her husband. McMaster observes that the children were spoiled by this intense, artificial care.

The doctor identifies Jake as a "loser" who was beyond redemption despite the family's efforts. McMaster describes Jake as a young man lacking a conscience, noting that he exhibited aggressive, rage-driven behavior toward smaller children during his youth. While McMaster was surprised that Jake committed murder, he explains that Jake’s temperament—specifically his tendency to incite others to violence during high-pressure situations like a robbery—aligns with the crime. Following this discussion, Calgary feels a sense of confusion and realization regarding the true nature of the case he is investigating.
